groups.title: Assignment Feedback
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Assignment Feedback Things that need to be done: • hand in book reports with the next assignment • check accuracy of the $21$ in the last section • give more $22$ • expand ideas to improve the grade

questions.title: check accuracy of the 【   】 in the last section

groups.title: Karen's Presentation
groups.desc:
Questions 23-27
Answer the questions below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.title: When will Karen give her presentation?
What must she do during the presentation?
By which date does she have to submit an abstract?
Where will the presentation take place?

groups.title: Modules Karen Will Study Next Year
groups.question_title: Which THREE modules will Karen study next year?

collect_option.title: Which THREE modules will Karen study next year?

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Oh, Karen.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Hi, come in, take a seat.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: I wanted to talk to you about this assignment you've handed in.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: I'll give it back to you with detailed feedback, but first there are a few things I'd like to run over.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: You know pointers for next time.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: When you hand in your next assignment, can you ensure that you've gone through these aspects?

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Hum, sure, will do.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Now, the first thing is your literature review.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: It was not sufficient enough so I would like to see your book reports to get your complete view.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: So can you submit those with the next assignment?

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Then I can find out which parts you've chosen to leave out.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Oh, yes.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Sorry, I didn't realise you needed them.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: It did cross my mind that what I've handed in is a bit thin, but then I started to work on another project and forgot to revise it.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: That's alright.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: And I've found some errors, just small ones where you had quoted people but not recorded the information properly at the end.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Don't forget to go through and make sure that your references are accurate.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: They were very relevant, though.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Just check and revise them using the format sample that I've emailed you beforehand, OK?

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Yes, I'll remember to check them.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Now, you made some good points but it might be helpful if you could include a few other examples, just to drive your point home.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Don't start writing more paragraphs; just slot them in at the end of what you've already written.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Now the thing you have to do, if you really want to get a better mark, is expand the ideas you're presenting.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Then your argument becomes more valid.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: OK.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Hum, can I talk to you about this presentation I've got to do?

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Sure, of course.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: When shall I stage what we've studied?

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Am I doing it next term?

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: I can't remember what we said.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Well, the thing is, Marco couldn't do it, so you agreed to do it at the next seminar.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: So how can I help?

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Um … actually, I'm wondering what you suggest to be the key part of the presentation.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: I mean, which part should I spend most of my time on?

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: That's a question to the point.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Well, you have very little time really, so it's absolutely essential for you to explain the experiment.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Of course, you'll have a summary in the handouts you give out but you can still start the presentation with that.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: It's optional, though.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: And do I have to give you the abstract first, or shall I just email it to all the students?

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Uh, no.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: I do need to see it first and see if it needs to be polished up.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: And then we'll get some printouts done.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Now, they'll need to be done by 3rd December, so I'll need to see it by 26th November if that's OK?

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Yes, fine.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Oh, and I need to talk to you about where the presentation is going to be made.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Given the actual number of participants and audience, we've had problems with the rooms because we'll need something bigger than usual.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: In our faculty, the only room available is the computer room, which is far from suitable considering the layout of the room, so we'll have to go across the road and do it in the chemistry lab.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: It's big enough to accommodate everyone and is equipped with the proper overhead projector in there as well.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: OK, right.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: And I get a grade for this, don't I?

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Yes, your first one was graded by your tutor, but this one will be scored by the professor.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: But relax, the criteria will be consistent, and you'll get an unbiased result.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Oh, and I've sorted out my modules for next year.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: You asked me to tell you about my choices.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: Yes, what've you decided?

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Oh, it was really hard to determine.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Hum, I've already done the Data Collection once, so that wasn't really a choice.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: I couldn't make up my mind between Language and Society and Communication Skills.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: I read the syllabuses but they sound more or less the same to me.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Anyway, I went for Communication Skills in the end because I know the lecturer.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Actually, Social Interaction seems to cover much the same ground, so I didn't bother with that either.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Hum, I thought Discourse Analysis looked really interesting.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: It studies and analyses the texts beyond the literal meaning of the sentences.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: It looks into the connotation beyond the sentences.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: And in fact, it also covers a little bit of Research Methodology in it, so I thought I'd do that, rather than the full methodology course.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: Kill two birds with one stone as it were.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: And then I fancied something drastically different, so I thought Psycholinguistics would be interesting, unless you think it'll be more worthwhile for me to do the Phonology course?

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: No, I think you've made sensible choices.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: I'm glad you're organised.

transcript.speaker: Course tutor
transcript.en: OK, let's meet again in a couple of weeks to see how you're getting on.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: OK, thank you.

transcript.speaker: Karen
transcript.en: See you then.

21.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 book reports、last section；提示词 the，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题定位词 last section 在录音中被替换为 at the end，比较容易错过，所以结合上一条已给信息中的定位词 book reports 和对话的推进更容易定位。在听到 book reports 时应集中注意力听导师要说的下一个问题，先是由并列信号词 And（此外）引导出问题，此处定位词 last section 的替换词出现，随后听到答案句："Don't forget to go through and make sure that your references are accurate."其中 go through 替换了题干中的 check，accurate 对应其名词形式 accuracy，不难识别，附近的名词即为答案。因此答案为 references。

22.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 give more，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题没有较好的定位词，注意理解对话并抓取合适的名词代入题干。在上一问题谈话结束之后，导师接着说作业中的另一个问题："Now, you made some good points but it might be helpful if you could include a few other examples, just to drive your point home."其中 include 替换了题干中的 give，a few other 替换了 more，其后名词即为答案。注意随后听到了干扰信息 more paragraphs，但通过开头的否定信号词 Don't 可排除。因此答案为 examples。

23.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 presentation；提示词 When，此处应填时间类名词。
题目解析：简答题这一题型在雅思听力中总体出现较少，难度不大，因为该题型的定位信息一般都比较明确，要填写的答案类型也比较明确。注意题目的字数要求，原文原词填写即可。本题先是听到定位词 presentation 出现，随后提示词和时间类名词 next term 出现，但由于是以问句形式出现，需继续往后听寻找肯定回复，导师回答："… so you agreed to do it at the next seminar."因此答案为 next seminar。

24.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 What must she do，此处应填动宾结构。
题目解析：本题与上一题的定位词重合，所以建议通过对话的推进来定位，寻找符合答案预判的动作信息。上一题结束之后，学生问 presentation 的 key part 是什么，注意导师的回答："… so it's absolutely essential for you to explain the experiment."其中 absolutely essential 的含义对应题干中的 must，符合预判的动作且比较合理的信息只有 explain the experiment。冠词 the 信息价值较弱，在符合题目字数要求的情况下，可填可不填。因此答案为 explain (the) experiment。

25.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 abstract；提示词 By which date，此处应填日期。
题目解析：本题比较容易定位，在听到 abstract 之后出现了两个日期，后一个日期以问句形式出现，但随后得到了学生的肯定答复。因此答案为 26(th) November 或 November 26(th)。

26.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 Where，此处应填地点类名词。
题目解析：本题虽没有明显的定位词，但答案类型方向比较明确，通过对话推进寻找地点类名词即可。导师先提到讨论下"where the presentation is going to be made"，预示答案信息即将出现，首先听到了 the computer room，不过以 far from suitable（完全不合适）予以了否定，接着听到了 the chemistry lab，说 big enough，学生给予肯定回答。因此答案为 (the) chemistry lab。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 grade；提示词 Who，此处应填表示人的名词。
题目解析：本题比较容易定位，定位词 grade 出现后，注意听导师的回答："… your first one was graded by your tutor, but this one will be scored by the professor."需要注意的是，tutor 用 first 和 was 限定，提示这是过去的情况，为干扰信息。因此答案为 (the) professor。

28.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 modules、next year；提示词 next year。
题目解析：题干中的 module 意为"课程模块"，8 个选项均为课程名称，不会发生替换，且它们均在录音中出现了，因此本部分的核心考点在于对干扰信息的否定识别。首先，听到了选项 B，随后以 wasn't 否定，可排除。其次，听到了选项 D 和选项 A 一起出现，逻辑上并列，但话锋一转说："Anyway, I went for Communication Skills in the end …"最终选择了 A，意味着否定了 D。接着，听到了选项 H，同样以否定表达 didn't bother 排除。而选项 C 出现时，则伴以积极态度信息 really interesting 予以了肯定，故正确。选项 G 紧跟着出现，不过以 rather than 予以否定。而后提及选项 F，同样用 interesting 展现了积极态度，故正确。最后，听到了选项 E，不过导师用 No 进行了否定。因此答案为 A & C & F。

听前预测：定位词 modules、next year；提示词 next year。
题目解析：题干中的 module 意为"课程模块"，8 个选项均为课程名称，不会发生替换，且它们均在录音中出现了，因此本部分的核心考点在于对干扰信息的否定识别。首先，听到了选项 B，随后以 wasn't 否定，可排除。其次，听到了选项 D 和选项 A 一起出现，逻辑上并列，但话锋一转说："Anyway, I went for Communication Skills in the end …"最终选择了 A，意味着否定了 D。接着，听到了选项 H，同样以否定表达 didn't bother 排除。而选项 C 出现时，则伴以积极态度信息 really interesting 予以了肯定，故正确。选项 G 紧跟着出现，不过以 rather than 予以否定。而后提及选项 F，同样用 interesting 展现了积极态度，故正确。最后，听到了选项 E，不过导师用 No 进行了否定。因此答案为 A & C & F。
